honoree.name: C. V. Whitney
honoree.summary: John Hay Whitney was born on August 17, 1904, and died on February 8, 1982. He was an American businessman, philanthropist, publisher, and diplomat who served as the United States Ambassador to the United Kingdom. Whitney was the owner of the New York Herald Tribune and a major financier of the Broadway musical South Pacific. He also served as a pilot in World War II and was a key patron of the Museum of Modern Art.

honoree.name: Corporal Jason Dunham
honoree.summary: Jason Dunham was a United States Marine who received the Medal of Honor for his actions during the Iraq War. He was born on November 10, 1981, in Scio, New York. He died on April 22, 2004, from wounds sustained in combat. He is remembered for his heroic sacrifice and has several military assets named in his honor.

honoree.name: Kurt Wyman
honoree.summary: Deputy Sheriff Kurt Wyman was shot and killed during a standoff following a domestic disturbance in Knoxboro. Officers responded to the scene of a domestic disturbance at approximately 9:00 pm when the man barricaded himself in his garage with a shotgun. Negotiators were attempting to get the man to surrender at approximately 2:00 am. Deputy Wyman was approaching the man with his taser when the subject opened fire, killing him. The suspect was shot in the exchange of gunfire and taken into custody. He was subsequently convicted of Deputy Wyman's murder and in April 2012 he was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole. Deputy Wyman was a U.S. Marine Corps veteran and had served with the Oneida County Sheriff's Office for four years. He is survived by his expectant wife and 18-month-old son. His wife went into labor upon being informed of Deputy Wyman's death and gave birth to his daughter.

honoree.name: The General Casimir Pulaski
honoree.summary: Casimir Pulaski was a Polish nobleman and military commander born on March 4, 1745, who became known as the Father of the American Cavalry for his service in the Continental Army during the American Revolutionary War. He fought in the Bar Confederation in Poland before traveling to the United States to support the Patriot cause. Pulaski organized the Pulaski Cavalry Brigade and saved George Washington's life at the Battle of Brandywine. He died on October 11, 1779, from wounds sustained in the Battle of Savannah.

honoree.name: Harriet Tubman
honoree.summary: Harriet Tubman was born into slavery in Maryland around 1822 and escaped to freedom in 1849. She became a famous conductor on the Underground Railroad, leading dozens of enslaved people to freedom and earning the nickname 'Moses'. During the American Civil War, she served the Union Army as a scout, spy, and nurse. After the war, she was a prominent activist in the women's suffrage movement.

honoree.name: Joe DiMaggio
honoree.summary: Joseph Paul DiMaggio was born on November 25, 1914, in Martinez, California, and died on March 8, 1999, in Hollywood, Florida. He was a professional baseball center fielder who played his entire career for the New York Yankees from 1936 to 1951. DiMaggio is most famous for his record-setting 56-game hitting streak in 1941 and winning nine World Series championships. He is widely regarded as one of the greatest baseball players in history and a major cultural icon of the 20th century.

honoree.name: Levon Helm
honoree.summary: Levon Helm was an American musician and actor born on May 26, 1939, in Elaine, Arkansas. He was the drummer and vocalist for the influential rock group The Band, known for classics like 'The Weight.' Helm also had a successful solo career, winning multiple Grammy Awards for his albums Dirt Farmer and Electric Dirt. He passed away on April 19, 2012, in New York City, leaving a lasting legacy in American music.

honoree.name: Trooper Shawn W. Snow
honoree.summary: Trooper Shawn Snow was electrocuted while assisting a driver changing a flat tire on an antique fire truck in Ogdensburg. As Trooper Snow and the truck's driver worked on the tire, the fire truck's ladder came in contact with overhead power lines. Trooper Snow and the truck driver were both killed instantly. Trooper Snow was a U.S. Coast Guard and a U.S. Navy veteran. He had served with the New York State Police for 19 years and was assigned to Troop B. He is survived by his wife and children.
